generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor component explicitly defined in BIM model with engineering parameters including measurement range and units, spatially aligned with physical assets in HVAC systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Humidity sensor component defined in BIM model with specified measurement capabilities, positioned according to spatial distribution requirements for environmental monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy sensor component modeled in BIM environment with geometric representation ensuring alignment with physical assets, supporting real-time occupancy monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption sensor component configured within BIM model to track building system energy usage, with spatial distribution matching physical infrastructure components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Data Transmitted
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric model derived from BIM representing building structures, spatial layouts, and components with semantic richness for AECO domain"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono
]

instance BehavioralControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavioral model created by Building Control Engineers within BIM environment representing sensor-actuator control logic and operational states"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance OperationalSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model generated via MDE transformations from enriched BIM models enabling simulation of building system performance and operational scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5
]

instance SensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of sensor data collected from physical assets including temperature, humidity, occupancy, and energy consumption measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono
]

instance ControlLogicConfiguration : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Configuration data defining control logic parameters and operational constraints for actuators and automated systems within the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuatorCommandDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data ingestion platform enabling secure, scalable IoT data connectivity for physical assets with bidirectional synchronization capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State management and event-driven service ensuring consistent digital twin state updates across distributed systems with semantic integration capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelDrivenDeploymentService
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity visualization and simulation engine providing interactive 3D environments for monitoring and analyzing digital twins with real-time rendering capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MDE framework automating digital twin deployment from BIM models through template-based configurations and rule-driven transformations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelDrivenDeploymentService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous bidirectional data exchange service between physical assets and digital twins with sub-second latency for asset-state updates and automated reconciliation"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight, OperationalStateAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive real-time visualization service providing rich interactive 3D representations of building components and their relationships with performance analytics overlays"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpatialAnalysisInsight
]

instance ModelDrivenDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated digital twin deployment service reducing manual intervention through reproducible configurations and domain-specific extensions for AECO workflows"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentOptimizationInsight, ConfigurationValidationAction
]

instance ExtensibilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Plug-and-play integration service enabling seamless addition of new sensors, actuators, or simulation engines through modular architecture extension points"
    DTDFVocab:provides ScalabilityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of operational anomalies including temperature spikes, structural vibrations, or equipment malfunctions through event-driven monitoring"
]

instance OperationalStateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of physical system operational states based on digital twin synchronization and control logic parameters"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpatialAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D spatial analysis of building components and their relationships enabling performance analytics and occupancy pattern visualization"
]

instance CollaborativeDecisionMakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Multi-user collaborative environment enabling stakeholders to interact with digital twin visualizations for shared decision-making processes"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeploymentOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of deployment efficiency identifying opportunities for reduced manual intervention and improved configuration reproducibility"
]

instance ConfigurationValidationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated validation of digital twin configurations against domain-specific requirements and operational constraints"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScalabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of system scalability capabilities supporting horizontal and vertical expansion for large-scale infrastructure projects"
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Integration of third-party custom services through defined extension points without modifying core system architecture"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component modeled within BIM environment enabling bidirectional communication with digital twin for remote control of HVAC systems including dampers and valves"
]

instance LightingController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting control actuator integrated as deployable component facilitating remote adjustment of lighting systems based on digital twin operational states"
]

instance ActuatorCommandDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACActuator, LightingController
]

// C1: Environments
instance BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical and virtual environment encompassing entire building infrastructure including structural elements, MEP systems, and operational components"
    base:contains BIMGeometricModel, BehavioralControlModel, OperationalSimulationModel,
                  TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyConsumptionSensor,
                  HVACActuator, LightingController
]

instance DigitalTwinRuntimeEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Runtime environment hosting digital twin services and enablers with configuration management capabilities for dynamic updates"
    base:contains EclipseHono, EclipseDitto, UnrealEngine5, ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework,
                  RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService, ModelDrivenDeploymentService,
                  ExtensibilityService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT framework enables **bidirectional control** through mechanisms that feed **control signals** back to Building Control Systems (BCS) for real-time adjustments. The framework supports **interactive visualization** (e.g., Unreal Engine interfaces) for monitoring and control, though specific **command types** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, emergency shutdowns) are not enumerated. **Control message formats** are not explicitly stated, but the framework’s **modular architecture** suggests support for extensible command structures. **Validation and authentication** mechanisms are implied through the framework’s design for secure bidirectional communication, though specifics (e.g., OAuth, TLS) are absent. **Feedback systems** are integrated to confirm command execution, ensuring alignment between digital commands and physical responses. **Conflict resolution** is addressed via runtime configuration management, allowing adjustments without full redeployment."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical building assets and their digital counterparts, eliminating temporal discrepancies. Synchronization is achieved through **live bidirectional data streams** without explicit mention of granularity intervals (e.g., sub-second, millisecond, or second-level precision). The framework supports **dynamic operational monitoring** with **low-latency requirements** implied by the term 'real-time,' though no specific latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms, <1s) are documented. Temporal granularity is dictated by the **operational needs of the AECO domain**, where **live updates** (e.g., sensor readings, control commands) must align with physical system dynamics without predefined constraints on frequency resolution."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **centralized hub-and-spoke architecture**, where the **DT Framework** orchestrates **coordinated roles and system components** to enable a **holistic approach to building lifecycle management**. Key architectural patterns include:
   - **Bidirectional data exchange** between physical assets and digital models, facilitated by **standardized extension points** for custom services.
   - **Runtime configuration management** to enable **adaptive adjustments** without full redeployment, ensuring **modularity** and **extensibility**.
   - **Component integration** through **service-oriented design**, allowing **DT Engineers** to extend capabilities dynamically (e.g., adding analytics, predictive maintenance, or IoT integrations).
   - **Resource management** is implied via the **scalability requirements** (NF_X) to handle heterogeneous systems, ensuring efficient allocation of computational and data resources.
   The system does not explicitly mention **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Kafka) or **specific coordination protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) but emphasizes **system-wide coordination** through the **central hub’s role** in managing bidirectional data flows and runtime adaptability."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) in the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain spans a **complete building life cycle**, from **initial design and conceptualization** to **construction, operation, refurbishment, and eventual decommissioning**. Each phase is characterized by distinct **representation types** and **transition management** challenges:

1. **Design Phase**: Leverages **model-driven frameworks** (e.g., BIM-based workflows) to create high-fidelity digital representations of assets, enabling real-time synchronization between physical and virtual models. **Transition management** between design and construction relies on **consistent data schemas** but remains fragmented due to lack of standardized integration.

2. **Construction Phase**: Employs **sensor networks** and **IoT devices** for real-time monitoring of modular construction components, though interoperability between systems remains limited. **Workflow automation** is critical for maintaining consistency across phases but is hindered by **proprietary ecosystems** (e.g., Bentley’s iTwin.js) and **manual configuration** requirements.

3. **Operation Phase**: Focuses on **life cycle management**, predictive maintenance, and cost modeling through **bidirectional communication** between physical assets and digital twins. **Governance approaches** are ad-hoc, lacking unified engineering workflows and stakeholder alignment, which exacerbates scalability issues.

4. **Refurbishment/Adaptation**: Requires **modular updates** to the digital twin to reflect physical changes, but current implementations lack **scalable, automated transition protocols**. **Decommissioning** phases are not explicitly addressed in the literature, highlighting gaps in end-of-life governance frameworks.

The **absence of unified governance** across phases is a key limitation, as it prevents seamless transitions between stages and hinders the adoption of standardized **asset life cycle management** practices.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The framework addresses **model fidelity** by ensuring **semantic consistency** between BIM-based static representations and dynamic Digital Twin artifacts through **MDE-driven transformations**. **Validation** is implicitly supported via **automated semantic checks** during model extraction, though explicit methods like 'Automated Semantic Validation' or 'machine learning-based anomaly detection' are not detailed. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated through **real-time synchronization** between physical and digital systems, but quantification methods are not specified. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are inferred from the framework’s emphasis on **bidirectional communication** and **modular architecture**, allowing custom plugins to address **semantic drift** or inconsistencies. **Quality assurance processes** are embedded in the **life cycle management** of the Digital Twin, ensuring deployable artifacts adhere to predefined semantic and functional requirements."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** as middleware for bidirectional communication, facilitating interoperability across heterogeneous infrastructure environments. Communication protocols are not explicitly named (e.g., MQTT, HTTP), but the framework supports **real-time data exchange** with unspecified bandwidth and latency requirements. Network reliability and redundancy mechanisms are not detailed, nor are specific security protocols (e.g., TLS, OAuth). The system emphasizes **interoperability** and **scalability** across components but lacks explicit technical specifications for network architecture or security compliance."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT framework is characterized by a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems. **Hosting platforms** are not explicitly specified (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise), but the framework’s scalability and runtime configuration management imply flexibility in deployment models. **Computational and storage requirements** are not detailed, though the mention of 'low-overhead updates' suggests efficient resource utilization. **Scalability mechanisms** are emphasized, allowing the DT to evolve with operational needs without full redeployment. **Deployment models** are not enumerated (e.g., Kubernetes, serverless), but the framework’s modularity supports incremental deployment. **Availability and reliability assurance** are addressed through runtime configuration management, ensuring continuous operation and adaptability to changing requirements. Visualization is supported via **Unreal Engine**, but infrastructure specifics (e.g., NoSQL databases) are not mentioned."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which address data exchange protocols and dependencies on external systems. The framework integrates with **generic BIM tools** (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) for device modeling and initial deployment, enabling seamless data exchange with **external sensors and IoT devices**. However, specific **interoperability standards** (e.g., ISO 19650, IFC) or **communication protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) are not explicitly documented. The system relies on **standardized extension points** to integrate with enterprise systems and other Digital Twins, though the exact integration patterns (e.g., REST APIs, message brokers) remain unspecified. The focus is on **scalable, low-overhead interoperability** to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational data."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The AECO Digital Twin ecosystem lacks **unified standardization frameworks**, with most existing frameworks (e.g., **Eclipse Ditto**, **BaSyx**) designed for **industrial or IoT applications** rather than AECO-specific needs. These frameworks provide **standard compliance** generically but do not adhere to **AECO-specific standards** like **BIM (Building Information Modeling)** or **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)**. The text explicitly states that **most frameworks are not designed with AECO standards in mind**, requiring **substantial manual effort** for adaptation.

**Interoperability standards** are not explicitly listed in the documents, though the absence of **standardized data schemas** and **integration frameworks** is highlighted as a key challenge. **Certification requirements** and **compliance frameworks** are not mentioned, and the lack of **proprietary ecosystem dependencies** (e.g., Bentley’s iTwin.js) further exacerbates the **fragmentation** of DT implementations. The documents emphasize that **AECO-specific adaptations** of existing frameworks are limited, necessitating **reengineering of system integration** to achieve **scalable, interoperable DT solutions**.
"
]
